task_completion.description: Generate the new random keyfile named 'lab_access_v2.key'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the VeraCrypt Keyfile Generator creating 'lab_access_v2.key', or a terminal/file manager window confirming the new file exists in '/home/ga/Keyfiles/'.

task_completion.description: Select the target volume for a credentials update
task_completion.visual_evidence: VeraCrypt main window or 'Change Volume Password' dialog showing the target volume path '/home/ga/Volumes/lab_data.hc' selected.

task_completion.description: Provide the current password and compromised keyfile
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Change Volume Password' dialog showing password fields filled (asterisks) in the 'Current' section, and the 'Current Keyfiles' list containing '/home/ga/Keyfiles/lab_access_compromised.key'.

task_completion.description: Configure the new keyfile while keeping the password
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Change Volume Password' dialog showing password fields filled in the 'New' section, and the 'New Keyfiles' list containing ONLY '/home/ga/Keyfiles/lab_access_v2.key' (the old keyfile must be removed from this list).

task_completion.description: Complete the in-place update and ensure the volume is dismounted
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the volume password/keyfiles were successfully changed, AND the VeraCrypt main window showing no volumes mounted in the drive slots.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the actual VeraCrypt application or valid CLI commands to perform the update.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the VeraCrypt GUI or terminal commands interacting with the 'veracrypt' binary, not just a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent performed an in-place header update rather than creating a new volume.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use 'Volume Tools -> Change Volume Password' (or equivalent CLI flag). The 'VeraCrypt Volume Creation Wizard' should NOT be used to overwrite or recreate 'lab_data.hc'.

integrity.description: Verify the new keyfile was generated randomly and not just typed out.
integrity.visual_evidence: Use of VeraCrypt's Keyfile Generator tool, or system tools like 'dd' with '/dev/urandom', rather than opening a text editor and typing random characters.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Volume: /home/ga/Volumes/lab_data.hc. Password: 'Research2024!'. Old Keyfile: /home/ga/Keyfiles/lab_access_compromised.key. New Keyfile: /home/ga/Keyfiles/lab_access_v2.key. The task requires an in-place update using VeraCrypt's 'Change Volume Password' feature (which handles keyfile rotation), not creating a new volume container.
